title_en: The Status of Women in Islam
content_en:
Muhammad Ali Qutb opens his survey of the women closest to the Prophet by establishing the theological and social framework within which their lives must be understood. The status of women in Islam represents one of the most contested and frequently misrepresented topics in contemporary discourse, and a clear presentation of the Islamic position — based on Quran and Sunnah rather than cultural distortions or apologetic oversimplifications — is essential before individual biographies can be properly appreciated.

The Quran's foundational statement on gender is one of striking equality in spiritual standing: 'Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women, the believing men and believing women, the obedient men and obedient women, the truthful men and truthful women... for them Allah has prepared forgiveness and a great reward' (33:35). This verse, which lists ten pairs of spiritual qualities with both masculine and feminine forms, establishes that the spiritual path to Allah is identical for men and women. There are no gender-specific gates to Paradise, no spiritual advantages or disadvantages deriving from biological sex.

At the same time, Islam acknowledges biological and social differences between men and women that it addresses through differentiated but complementary roles. The Quran assigns men the primary responsibility of material provision (qiwamah), while women's primary sphere is identified as the management of the home and the nurturing of children. These roles are described as complementary contributions to a shared enterprise — the building of a family and, through families, a society — rather than as expressions of superiority or inferiority.

The Prophet's transformative impact on women's social status in Arabia was revolutionary by any historical standard. He prohibited female infanticide — a practice whose condemnation the Quran expresses in the most morally graphic terms (81:8-9). He established women's right to inheritance, their right to own and manage property, their right to consent to marriage, and their right to seek divorce when conditions became intolerable. He established the mahr (bridal gift) as the woman's exclusive property, not a payment to her family. He made seeking religious knowledge obligatory for women as well as men.

Qutb's introduction also addresses the common misunderstanding that Islam's regulations about dress, public conduct, and gender interaction are restrictions on women. The Islamic framework, properly understood, restricts men's behavior (gaze, unsolicited contact, harassment) as much as women's, and the purpose of the regulations is protection of the social fabric and the dignity of all parties, not the subordination of women to men. The women who surrounded the Prophet — his wives, daughters, and female Companions — lived this framework as liberation from the actual restrictions of pre-Islamic society, not as a new system of constraint.
content_id:
Muhammad Ali Qutb membuka surveinya tentang perempuan-perempuan yang paling dekat dengan Nabi ﷺ dengan menetapkan kerangka teologis dan sosial yang menjadi landasan pemahaman kehidupan mereka. Kedudukan perempuan dalam Islam merupakan salah satu topik yang paling banyak diperdebatkan dan kerap disalahpahami dalam wacana kontemporer, sehingga diperlukan paparan yang jelas tentang posisi Islam — berdasarkan Al-Quran dan Sunnah, bukan distorsi budaya atau penyederhanaan apologetik — sebelum biografi-biografi individual dapat diapresiasi dengan semestinya.

Pernyataan mendasar Al-Quran tentang gender adalah persamaan yang mencolok dalam kedudukan spiritual: "Sesungguhnya laki-laki dan perempuan yang muslim, laki-laki dan perempuan yang mukmin, laki-laki dan perempuan yang taat, laki-laki dan perempuan yang benar, ... Allah telah menyediakan untuk mereka ampunan dan pahala yang besar" (QS. 33:35). Ayat ini, yang menyebutkan sepuluh pasang sifat spiritual dalam bentuk maskulin dan feminin, menegaskan bahwa jalan spiritual menuju Allah adalah sama bagi laki-laki maupun perempuan. Tidak ada pintu surga yang khusus berdasarkan jenis kelamin, tidak ada keuntungan atau kerugian spiritual yang berasal dari jenis kelamin biologis.

Pada saat yang sama, Islam mengakui perbedaan biologis dan sosial antara laki-laki dan perempuan, yang ditangani melalui peran yang berbeda namun saling melengkapi. Al-Quran menetapkan laki-laki sebagai penanggung jawab utama dalam hal nafkah (qiwamah), sementara ranah utama perempuan diidentifikasikan sebagai pengelolaan rumah tangga dan pengasuhan anak. Peran-peran ini digambarkan sebagai kontribusi yang saling melengkapi dalam satu usaha bersama — membangun keluarga dan, melalui keluarga, membangun masyarakat — bukan sebagai ekspresi superioritas atau inferioritas.

Dampak transformatif Nabi ﷺ terhadap status sosial perempuan di Arabia sungguh revolusioner menurut standar sejarah mana pun. Beliau melarang praktik pembunuhan bayi perempuan — suatu praktik yang penghukumannya oleh Al-Quran diungkapkan dengan ungkapan moral yang sangat kuat (QS. 81:8-9). Beliau menetapkan hak perempuan atas warisan, hak memiliki dan mengelola harta, hak untuk memberikan persetujuan dalam pernikahan, dan hak untuk mencari cerai ketika kondisi menjadi tidak tertahankan. Beliau menetapkan mahar sebagai hak milik eksklusif perempuan, bukan pembayaran kepada keluarganya. Beliau menjadikan pencarian ilmu agama sebagai kewajiban bagi perempuan maupun laki-laki.

Pendahuluan Qutb juga membahas kesalahpahaman umum bahwa regulasi Islam tentang pakaian, perilaku di ruang publik, dan interaksi antar jenis kelamin merupakan pembatasan terhadap perempuan. Kerangka Islam, jika dipahami dengan benar, membatasi perilaku laki-laki (pandangan, kontak tanpa izin, pelecehan) sama seperti membatasi perempuan, dan tujuan regulasi tersebut adalah melindungi tatanan sosial dan martabat semua pihak, bukan menundukkan perempuan kepada laki-laki. Perempuan-perempuan yang mengelilingi Nabi ﷺ — para istri, putri, dan sahabat perempuan beliau — menghayati kerangka ini sebagai pembebasan dari pembatasan nyata dalam masyarakat pra-Islam, bukan sebagai sistem pembatasan yang baru.
content_ar: محمد ﷺ علي Qutb opens his survey of the women closest to النبي ﷺ by establishing the theological and social framework within which their lives must be understood. The status of women in الإسلام represents one of the most contested and frequently misrepresented topics in contemporary discourse, and a clear presentation of the Islamic position — based on القرآن الكريم and السنة rather than cultural distortions or apologetic oversimplifications — is الجوهري قبل individual biographies can be properly appreciated. The القرآن الكريم's foundational statement on gender is one of striking equality in spiritual standing: 'Indeed, the مسلم men and مسلم women, the believing men and believing women, the obedient men and obedient women, the truthful men and truthful women... for them الله has prepared المغفرة and a great الأجر' (33:35). This الآية, which lists ten pairs of spiritual qualities with both masculine and feminine forms, establishes that the spiritual الطريق to الله is identical for men and women. There are no gender-specific gates to الجنة, no spiritual advantages or disadvantages deriving from biological sex. At the same time, الإسلام acknowledges biological and social differences between men and women that it addresses through differentiated but complementary roles. The القرآن الكريم assigns men the الأساسي المسؤولية of material provision (qiwamah), بينما women's الأساسي sphere is identified as the management of the home and the nurturing of الأولاد. These roles are described as complementary contributions to a shared enterprise — the building of a الأسرة and, through families, a المجتمع — rather than as expressions of superiority or inferiority. النبي ﷺ's transformative impact on women's social status in Arabia was revolutionary by any historical standard. He prohibited female infanticide — a practice whose condemnation the القرآن الكريم expresses in the most morally graphic terms (81:8-9). He established women's الحق to inheritance, their الحق to own and manage property, their الحق to consent to marriage, and their الحق to seek divorce عندما conditions became intolerable. He established the mahr (bridal الهبة) as the woman's exclusive property, not a payment to her الأسرة. He made seeking religious العلم obligatory for women as well as men. Qutb's introduction أيضاً addresses the common misunderstanding that الإسلام's regulations about dress, public السلوك, and gender interaction are restrictions on women. The Islamic framework, properly understood, restricts men's السلوك (gaze, unsolicited contact, harassment) as much as women's, and the purpose of the regulations is protection of the social fabric and the الكرامة of all parties, not the subordination of women to men. The women who surrounded النبي ﷺ — his wives, daughters, and female الصحابة — lived this framework as liberation from the actual restrictions of pre-Islamic المجتمع, not as a new system of constraint.
